title: SEG Generator Tour
description: Guided walkthrough of rings, stator windings, flux lines, and the ionization torus.

steps.title: Welcome to the SEG lab
steps.body: This instrument models the Searl Effect Generator — three concentric roller rings, stator guides, and pickup coils. Energy pipes link it to companion physics demos in the overview.

steps.title: Enter the generator
steps.body: We focus on the SEG unit. Rollers orbit in the horizontal plane; the central shaft carries the bearing stack. The 2D plan view (D key) mirrors this motion.

steps.title: Inner roller ring
steps.body: Innermost NdFeB/Cu rollers — 10× in the Searl 10/25/35 preset. Pole bands alternate N/S for magnetic coupling to the stator.

steps.title: Stator rings & separators
steps.body: Copper stator bands guide each roller orbit. Insulated ring separators maintain the air gap — typically ~3 mm in literature-derived layouts.

steps.title: Outer roller ring
steps.body: The outer ring carries the highest roller count (35× Searl). Toroidal motion feeds energy into pickup coils at the perimeter.

steps.title: Pickup coils (C-cores)
steps.body: 24 outer pickup coils sense passing rollers. Coil energy lights up in the operator panel as RPM rises — a simplified EMF induction model.

steps.title: Magnetic flux (RK4)
steps.body: WebGPU path traces bidirectional flux lines via RK4 integration around roller rings. Try the B-field experiment slider to see flux and particles respond.

steps.title: Ionization torus
steps.body: At high spin (press START), corona particles form a toroidal sheath — an educational air-breakdown proxy, not a claim of aneutronic fusion.

steps.title: Compare layouts
steps.body: Searl 10/25/35 vs Roschin–Godin 12-roller vs Legacy 8/12/16 — use SEG Layout presets in the sidebar. Share your experiment with Copy Lab Link.
steps.layoutHint: Try Roschin preset from the sidebar
